body.title: [sprint] Standardize ~/.unseen_university/devices/ to flat class+instance sibling dirs, consistent naming
body.intention: I intend that a device's runtime-data dirs under ~/.unseen_university/devices/ are FLAT SIBLINGS — the class dir and each instance dir side by side, not nested — with a consistent naming convention, so the data folder is easy to see and traverse.
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-03: seeing `devices/DickSimnel` and `devices/DS.0` side by side, he prefers the FLAT layout (class dir + instance dir as siblings) over nesting (NOT `devices/DickSimnel/DS.0`). Same for Igor: `devices/igor` + `devices/IgorWild1` (NOT `devices/igor/IgorWild1`, reversing an earlier nested example). Reason: easier to see/traverse.

Current on-disk (~/.unseen_university/devices/): `DickSimnel`+`DS.0` already flat-sibling (good), but `cc_0` and `granny_weatherwax` are inconsistent (casing + instance-naming: cc_0 vs DS.0; granny_weatherwax vs DickSimnel). Standardize the convention and reconcile existing dirs to it. Decide+document the canonical form: class dir = <ClassName>, instance dir = <Abbrev>.<n> (e.g. DS.0) or the class's instance_name — reconcile with IdentityMixin.instance_name (capabilities/identity.py) and D-worker-instance-identity so the on-disk dir matches what the code computes.

**Affected files:** whatever code resolves a device's runtime-data dir under uu_home()/devices/ (grep 'devices' in _uu_root.py / device base / pool.py); the on-disk dirs themselves (migrate cc_0/granny_weatherwax to convention).
**Design rules:** check-new-paths-unseen-university (confirm dir moves under ~/.unseen_university); log-every-state-change (device data-dir resolution).
**Scope boundary:** IN — define+document the flat class+instance sibling convention, make the code resolve dirs that way, reconcile existing inconsistent dirs (cc_0, granny_weatherwax). OUT — the identity/naming semantics themselves (D-worker-instance-identity); de-Discworld renaming (D-agent-subclass-and-de-discworld).
**Test plan:** integration — a device resolves its runtime-data dir to the flat sibling path; class and instance dirs are siblings (red: nested or inconsistent; green: flat + convention-named). Verify no code hardcodes the old cc_0/granny_weatherwax paths.
**Completion criteria:** devices/ uses flat class+instance sibling dirs with one documented naming convention; existing dirs reconciled; code resolves to the flat path.
**Rollback:** git revert the resolver change; restore moved dirs from snapshot.
